privileged_info_summary: The VA Lung Cancer Trial dataset contains 137 observations of male lung cancer patients from a randomized trial comparing standard vs test chemotherapy. Key variables include treatment assignment, Karnofsky performance score, cell type, and survival time. Cox proportional hazards regression reveals that Karnofsky score is a highly significant predictor with a hazard ratio of 0.968 (p<0.001), meaning each unit increase in Karnofsky score reduces hazard by 3.2%. Treatment assignment shows a hazard ratio of approximately 1.34 but is not statistically significant (p=0.156). The actual treatment HR contradicts the metadata claim of 1.02. Cell type variables (especially adenocarcinoma and small cell) are significant predictors of survival.
